note: RMSF values are in relative units (normalized to mean=1.0). ANM spring constant γ is set uniformly; absolute magnitudes are not comparable to MD-RMSF in Å. Fold-change and DCCM values are γ-independent.

aoh_pocket_analysis.interpretation: Pocket residues are 14.3% LESS flexible than structural background (fold-change 0.857) in the apo crystal structure 1W60. This is consistent with a buried, rigidly packed cryptic pocket that is not accessible in the apo state and requires ligand-induced conformational change to open. A fold-change < 1 is expected for true cryptic pockets (vs allosteric pockets, which show elevated apo RMSF).
aoh_pocket_analysis.dccm_interpretation: Internal DCCM = 0.0995 — mild positive correlation among AOH1996 pocket residues in the 20 lowest-frequency ANM modes. This indicates the residues move as a somewhat coherent unit despite being rigid, consistent with a concerted opening mechanism.

method_note: ANM is a validated fast alternative to MD for flexibility analysis. ANM RMSF correlates with MD RMSF at r~0.6-0.8 (Eyal et al. 2006, Proteins 63:1072). Absolute RMSF values are in normalized units (mean=1.0). Fold-changes and DCCM are dimensionless.
